44	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
45	
46	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
47	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
48	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
49	
50	namespace PresheafOfModules
51	
52	open InternalHom Opposite
53	
54	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
55	
56	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
57	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
58	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
59	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
60	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
61	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
62	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
63	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
64	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
65	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
66	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
67	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
68	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
69	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
70	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
71	  exact
72	    { toFun := fun φ =>
73	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
74	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
75	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
76	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
77	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
78	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
79	      invFun := fun r =>
80	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
81	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
82	      left_inv := fun φ => by
83	        ext Y
84	        dsimp only
85	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
86	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
87	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
88	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
89	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
90	        rfl
91	      right_inv := fun r => by
92	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
93	            (restr X.unop
94	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
95	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
96	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
97	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
98	        exact mul_one r }
99	
100	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
101	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
102	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
103	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
104	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
105	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
106	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
107	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
108	    (fun {X Y} f => by
109	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
110	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
111	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
112	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
113	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
114	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
115	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
116	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
117	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
118	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
119	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
120	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
121	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
122	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
123	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
124	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
125	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
126	      rw [hrm] at key
127	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
128	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
129	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
130	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
131	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
132	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
133	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
134	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
135	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
136	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
137	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
138	
139	end PresheafOfModules

147	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
148

1	# Analogy: pushforward-along-a-ring-iso commutes with the presheaf dual `ℋom(–,𝟙_)`
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	dual252
8	
9	## Iteration
10	252
11	
12	## Question
13	For the Step-4 residual of `dual_restrict_iso` (`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean:254`):
14	```
15	(pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val) ≅ PresheafOfModules.dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)
16	```
17	(`β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring iso `𝒪_Y(V) ≅ 𝒪_X(fV)`):
18	1. Does Mathlib have an idiom for "pushforward along a sectionwise ring iso commutes with the internal-hom dual" for `PresheafOfModules`?
19	2. What is the precise `isoMk` skeleton, mirroring the closed H2 step of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`?
20	3. Is `InternalHom.restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` the right ingredient, or is there a more direct `restrictScalars`-vs-`dual` commutation in Mathlib?
21	
22	## Project artifact(s)
23	- `DualInverse.lean:228-254` — `dual_restrict_iso`; one `sorry` at the Step-4 presheaf residual.
24	- `PresheafInternalHom.lean:234-266` — `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the proposed atom; CLOSED, axiom-clean).
25	- `PresheafInternalHom.lean:893-896` — `PresheafOfModules.dual M = internalHom M 𝟙_` (slice internal hom).
26	- `PresheafInternalHom.lean:628-674` — `homModule`/`internalHomObjModule`: dual section value `(dual M).obj (op U) = (restr U M ⟶ restr U 𝟙_)`, a **slice Hom over `Over U`**, NOT a single linear dual.
27	- `PresheafInternalHom.lean:504-513` — `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective` (the H2 atom of the *tensor* lane, for comparison).
28	
29	## Decisions identified
30	
31	### Decision 1: Does Mathlib supply the pushforward-vs-dual idiom for `PresheafOfModules`?
32	
33	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE. Mathlib has `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` / `pushforward`
34	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward`) and the sheaf-level
35	  `SheafOfModules.pushforward*`, but **no internal hom, no dual, and no `MonoidalClosed`
36	  instance for `PresheafOfModules`** — verified: `loogle "MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules ?R)"`
37	  returns no results. The entire `PresheafOfModules.dual = ℋom(–,𝟙_)` slice construction
38	  (`internalHom`, `homModule`, `restr`) is project-local. So there is *nothing upstream to
39	  commute pushforward against*.
40	- **Project's path**: build the iso by hand sectionwise.
41	- **Gap**: divergent-and-necessary (the upstream object does not exist).
42	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (expected; the gap is genuinely upstream).
43	
44	### Decision 2: Is the prover's "close sectionwise via `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`" faithful to the tensor lane's H2?
45	
46	- **Mathlib idiom for the *shape***: the abstract route does exist —
47	  `CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Rigid.OfEquivalence.hasRightDualOfEquivalence`
48	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Rigid.OfEquivalence`) transports a right dual along a
49	  strong-monoidal equivalence. But it requires the project's `dual` to *be* the rigid-category
50	  right dual of a registered `MonoidalCategory` + rigid structure on `PresheafOfModules`, which
51	  does not exist. Porting cost is prohibitive — not the path.
52	- **Why the tensor-lane analogy is NOT faithful**: the H2 step of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`
53	  worked because **Mathlib's `tensorObj` is sectionwise**: `(M ⊗ N)(X) = M(X) ⊗ N(X)`, so the
54	  presheaf tensorator of `restrictScalars β` reduces, section by section, to
55	  `ModuleCat.restrictScalars`'s tensorator (= `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`), packaged as
56	  `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`. **The project `dual` is NOT sectionwise**: its value
57	  `(dual M)(U) = (restr U M ⟶ restr U 𝟙_)` is a Hom of presheaves over the *whole down-set*
58	  `Over U`, not a fiber at `U`. So there is no clean sectionwise reduction of "pushforward
59	  commutes with dual" to a single `ModuleCat`-level fact.
60	- **The actual decomposition of the residual** (at section `V : (Opens Y)ᵒᵖ`):
61	  ```
62	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V
63	      = restrictScalars (β.app V) ( Hom_{Over fV}(restr fV M.val, restr fV 𝟙_X) )   -- 𝒪_X(fV)→𝒪_Y(V)
64	  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
65	      =                          Hom_{Over V} (restr V (pushβ M.val), restr V 𝟙_Y)    -- over 𝒪_Y(V)
66	  ```
67	  Going from the first to the second couples **two** transports that `tensorObj` never needed:
68	  - **(B) ring-iso reconciliation of the unit codomain** `𝒪_X(fV) ≅ 𝒪_Y(V)` — THIS is exactly
69	    what `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` does (its codomain is hardcoded to the ground ring =
70	    the monoidal unit, matching `dual`'s `𝟙_`). ✓ right atom.
71	  - **(A) slice-site transport** of the *domain* presheaf: `restr V (pushβ M.val)` over
72	    `Over V` (in `Opens Y`) ↔ `restr fV M.val` over `Over fV` (in `Opens X`), under the
73	    fully-faithful `f.opensFunctor` restricted to the down-set of `fV`. This is a genuine,
74	    NON-sectionwise build. `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` does NOT touch it; the directive's
75	    banned `overSliceSheafEquiv` was the natural-but-wrong-level tool for it (Sheaf cat / fixed
76	    value cat). The dual's slice-Hom is NOT a single linear dual (the `restr U M ⟶ restr U 𝟙_`
77	    carries down-set-wide compatibility data beyond the terminal-section functional
78	    `M(U) →ₗ 𝒪(U)`), so (A) cannot be elided.
79	- **Gap**: the prover's framing ("sectionwise via `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`")
80	  **under-scopes** by omitting leg (A). `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` is leg (B) only.
81	- **Verdict**: DIVERGE/GAP — the residual needs (A)+(B); the atom supplies (B).
82	
83	### Decision 3: Is there a more direct `restrictScalars`-vs-`dual` `LinearEquiv` in Mathlib?
84	
85	- **Mathlib idiom**: NO. `LinearMap.restrictScalars` / `LinearEquiv.restrictScalars`
86	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LinearMap.Defs`, `Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Equiv.Basic`) are gated on
87	  `LinearMap.CompatibleSMul M N R S` — a **scalar-tower** situation (`R → S`, *same* modules,
88	  codomain unchanged). The dual swap `(M →ₗ[S] S) → (M →ₗ[R] R)` changes the codomain `S ↝ R`,
89	  which is only possible via a ring *isomorphism* `e : R ≃+* S` carrying `S` back to `R` — not a
90	  `CompatibleSMul` instance. Mathlib has no `Module.Dual`-vs-`restrictScalars` commutation of
91	  this codomain-swapping kind.
92	- **Project's path**: `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (CLOSED, axiom-clean). It is the
93	  correct and necessary atom; keep it.
94	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (project lemma is the right primitive).
95	
96	## Recommendation
97	
98	`restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` **is** the correct ring-iso atom (leg B) — the faithful
99	`ModuleCat`-level dual analogue of `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`, NOT of the *presheaf*-level
100	`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`. But the Step-4 residual is **not** sectionwise-closable by it
101	alone: it additionally needs the **slice-site transport (leg A)** identifying `restr V (pushβ M.val)`
102	over `Over V ⊂ Opens Y` with `restr fV M.val` over `Over fV ⊂ Opens X` across the open immersion —
103	the part `tensorObj_restrict_iso` never needed because `tensorObj` is sectionwise and `dual` is not.
104	
105	Concrete build (mirror of the H2 plumbing, swapping in the slice transport):
106	```
107	PresheafOfModules.isoMk
108	  (app := fun V => (sliceDualTransport f M V).toModuleIso)     -- 𝒪_Y(V)-LinearEquiv
109	  (naturality := fun {V W} g => /- thin poset Opens Y: Subsingleton.elim, as in dualUnitIsoGen -/)
110	```
111	where `sliceDualTransport f M V :`
112	```
113	  restrictScalars (β.app V) (restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X)
114	    ≃ₗ[𝒪_Y(V)] (restr V (pushβ M.val) ⟶ restr V 𝟙_Y)
115	```
116	is assembled as **(A) slice equivalence Hom-transport ≪ₗ (B) `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`**.
117	The load-bearing field is the construction of `sliceDualTransport` (leg A), NOT the outer
118	`isoMk` naturality (which is thin-poset-trivial, exactly as in `dualUnitIsoGen` /`dualIsoOfIso`).
119	
120	Leg (A) must be built at the **PresheafOfModules / varying-ring** level (the slice value of
121	`internalHom`), so it is a genuine new build — but it is a *down-set restriction of the open
122	immersion's fully-faithfulness*, not a missing Mathlib import. The planner should scope leg (A)
123	as a standalone verified lemma FIRST (a presheaf-level `restr`-vs-`f.opensFunctor` base-change /
124	Beck–Chevalley square over the slice), then compose with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`.
125	
126	Alternative worth weighing before committing to (A): derive `dual_restrict_iso` from the already
127	-closed `tensorObj_restrict_iso` by **uniqueness of monoidal inverses** (dual = ⊗-inverse), using
128	the eval/coeval naturality — this sidesteps the slice transport entirely if the inverse-uniqueness
129	glue is cheaper than leg (A). Flagged, not verified.
130

4803:\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}, the local-triviality of the dual
4805:\cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}, and the discharge remark
5342:  (\cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}): both reduce to identifying a
5384:  \label{lem:dual_restrict_iso}
5534:  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:dual_restrict_iso, lem:dual_unit_iso,
5554:  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:dual_restrict_iso, lem:dual_unit_iso,
5568:      \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}: restriction along the open immersion \(f\) commutes
5645:  (ii) of the A-bridge \cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}: once the
5654:  \label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
5655:  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}
5751:        lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat, lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict,
5767:  \cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat} --- to a single global morphism

5640	  is a thin wrapper that packages \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.homMk}\) at the scheme
5641	  level: the sectionwise-linearity datum promotes the ab-group morphism to a genuine
5642	  morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Its \(\mathtt{@[simp]}\) companion
5643	  \(\mathtt{toPresheaf\_map\_homMk}\) records that forgetting back recovers \(g\), i.e.\
5644	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,X).\mathtt{map}\,(\mathtt{homMk}\,g\,hg) = g\). This is step
5645	  (ii) of the A-bridge \cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}: once the
5646	  underlying ab-sheaf morphism has been glued, \(\mathtt{homMk}\) re-attaches the
5647	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linearity to produce the global morphism of modules.
5648	\end{definition}
5649	
5650	\begin{lemma}
5651	
5652	\leanok
5653	[Compatible local morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global morphism]
5654	  \label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
5655	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}
5656	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and \(\{U_i\}\) an
5657	  open cover of \(X\). Suppose given, for each \(i\), a morphism
5658	  \(f_i \colon M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_i\) such that
5659	  for all \(i, j\) the restrictions of \(f_i\) and \(f_j\) to \(U_i \cap U_j\) agree.
5660	  Then there is a unique global morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
5661	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) whose restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\).
5662	\end{lemma}
5663	
5664	\begin{proof}
5665	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_homMk, lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
5666	  A morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is, concretely, a morphism of the
5667	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups that is in addition \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear
5668	  sectionwise. We build \(f\) in two steps.
5669	
5670	  \emph{(i) Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.} Forget \(M\) and \(N\) to their
5671	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups. The morphisms-between-them presheaf
5672	  \(\mathcal{H} := \mathtt{presheafHom}\,(M.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf})\,
5673	  (N.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf})\), whose value at an open \(W\) is the abelian
5674	  group of morphisms \(M|_W \to N|_W\) of presheaves of abelian groups, is itself a
5675	  \emph{sheaf of types} --- this is the ``hom into a sheaf is a sheaf'' principle
5676	  \(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom}\), which consumes the sheaf condition of \(N\).
5677	  Regarding \(\mathcal{H}\) as a \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\), its universal gluing
5678	  property \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) amalgamates any compatible family of local
5679	  sections of \(\mathcal{H}\) into a unique global section. This route is preferred over
5680	  the raw sieve-level amalgamation \(\mathtt{presheafHom\_isSheafFor}\), which carries
5681	  strictly more bookkeeping.
5682	
5683	  The load-bearing datum is, for each \(i\), the \emph{local section}
5684	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i \in \mathcal{H}.\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,U_i)\)
5685	  manufactured from \(f_i\). Its component at a pair \((V, h : V \to U_i)\) is the given
5686	  component of \(f_i\), conjugated by the canonical \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) identifications
5687	  that arise from the open-set equality \(\iota_i\bigl(\iota_i^{-1}(V)\bigr) = V\) valid
5688	  for \(V \subseteq U_i\) (the image under the open immersion \(\iota_i : U_i \to X\) of
5689	  the preimage of \(V\) is \(V\) again). The genuine coherence risk of the whole
5690	  construction is concentrated in the \emph{naturality field} of
5691	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\): the requirement that these
5692	  \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugated components of \(f_i\) commute across the morphisms of
5693	  the slice category \(\mathtt{Over}\,U_i\). This is the one sub-piece that should be
5694	  built and verified first, as a standalone axiom-clean lemma.
5695	
5696	  This \(\mathtt{localSection}\) naturality is precisely the \emph{section-direction
5697	  slice} of the comparison packaged in \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}:
5698	  converting the local datum \(f_i\), which lives over the open-immersion pullback
5699	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,\widetilde{U_i}\), into a section of \(\mathcal{H}\) indexed over the
5700	  slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,U_i\) is the one-sided realisation of the open-immersion
5701	  \(\leftrightarrow\) slice transport. As there, the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugation is
5702	  controlled by the down-set identity \(\iota_i(\iota_i^{-1}(V)) = V\) for
5703	  \(V \subseteq U_i\), and the coherence is automatic on the thin poset
5704	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\). This A-engine uses the
5705	  sheaf-site equivalence \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}; the C-bridge
5706	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}, by contrast, uses a per-open slice comparison at
5707	  the presheaf level over the \emph{varying} section ring \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), and so
5708	  does \emph{not} reduce to the same sheaf-site root --- the two are independent and
5709	  must be built separately.
5710	
5711	  The remaining four sub-steps are mechanical: (a) the
5712	  cocycle/\(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) condition on the family
5713	  \((\mathtt{localSection}\,i)_i\), which is exactly the assumed agreement of \(f_i\)
5714	  and \(f_j\) on the overlaps \(U_i \cap U_j\); (b) gluing via
5715	  \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) and converting the amalgamated section over
5716	  \(\mathrm{op}\,\top\) into a genuine morphism \(g \colon M.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf}
5717	  \to N.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf}\) of the underlying presheaves --- the
5718	  \(\top\)-section-to-morphism passage being the global-sections equivalence
5719	  \(\mathtt{presheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) / \(\mathtt{sheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) of the
5720	  hom-sheaf \(\mathcal{H}\) (which, since \(\top\) is terminal in \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\),
5721	  lands the glued section as an honest morphism of presheaves), \emph{not} a hand-unfold
5722	  of the \(\top\)-section; (c) the
5723	  sectionwise linearity check; and (d) the recovery that the restriction of \(g\) to
5724	  each \(U_i\) is the ab-sheaf morphism underlying \(f_i\).
5725	
5726	  \emph{(ii) Promote to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear.} The linearity equation
5727	  \(g(r \cdot m) = r \cdot g(m)\) is a sectionwise identity between two sections of a
5728	  separated presheaf. It holds on each \(U_i\), because there \(g\) restricts to the
5729	  module morphism \(f_i\); since the two sides agree on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) and the
5730	  ambient presheaf is separated, they agree globally. Packaging \(g\) together with this
5731	  global linearity through the scheme-level wrapper \cref{def:scheme_modules_homMk}
5732	  (\(\mathtt{homMk}\)) yields a morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
5733	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\); its restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\) and its
5734	  uniqueness are both inherited from the ab-sheaf amalgamation of step (i).
5735	
5736	  This gluing engine is substantially larger than a routine glue: it is dominated by the
5737	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\) naturality field and the convert-and-recover bookkeeping, and
5738	  the naturality is the section-direction slice of
5739	  \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv} --- so its true cost is bounded below by
5740	  building that sheaf-site equivalence, not by the surrounding assembly. It is therefore to be
5741	  scoped as a multi-piece build --- with \(\mathtt{localSection}\), together with its
5742	  naturality, built as a standalone verified lemma first --- rather than a one-shot.
5743	  The step computes no tensor stalk --- it is purely a statement about gluing morphisms
5744	  of sheaves --- and so it does not re-enter the stalk-tensor commutation.
5745	\end{proof}

/-
Copyright (c) 2023 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Joël Riou
-/
module

public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Over

/-! Internal hom of sheaves

In this file, given two sheaves `F` and `G` on a site `(C, J)` with values
in a category `A`, we define a sheaf of types
`sheafHom F G` which sends `X : C` to the type of morphisms
between the restrictions of `F` and `G` to the categories `Over X`.

We first define `presheafHom F G` when `F` and `G` are
presheaves `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` and show that it is a sheaf when `G` is a sheaf.

TODO:
- turn both `presheafHom` and `sheafHom` into bifunctors
- for a sheaf of types `F`, the `sheafHom` functor from `F` is right-adjoint to
  the product functor with `F`, i.e. for all `X` and `Y`, there is a
  natural bijection `(X ⨯ F ⟶ Y) ≃ (X ⟶ sheafHom F Y)`.
- use these results in order to show that the category of sheaves of types is Cartesian closed

-/

@[expose] public section

universe v v' u u'

namespace CategoryTheory

open Category Opposite Limits

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
  {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A]

variable (F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)

/-- Given two presheaves `F` and `G` on a category `C` with values in a category `A`,
this `presheafHom F G` is the presheaf of types which sends an object `X : C`
to the type of morphisms between the "restrictions" of `F` and `G` to the category `Over X`. -/
@[simps! obj]
def presheafHom : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type _ where
  obj X := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ F ⟶ (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ G
  map f := TypeCat.ofHom (Functor.whiskerLeft (Over.map f.unop).op)
  map_id := by
    rintro ⟨X⟩
    ext φ ⟨Y⟩
    simpa [Over.mapId] using φ.naturality ((Over.mapId X).hom.app Y).op
  map_comp := by
    rintro ⟨X⟩ ⟨Y⟩ ⟨Z⟩ ⟨f : Y ⟶ X⟩ ⟨g : Z ⟶ Y⟩
    ext φ ⟨W⟩
    simpa [Over.mapComp] using φ.naturality ((Over.mapComp g f).hom.app W).op

variable {F G}

/-- Equational lemma for the presheaf structure on `presheafHom`.
It is advisable to use this lemma rather than `dsimp [presheafHom]` which may result
in the need to prove equalities of objects in an `Over` category. -/
lemma presheafHom_map_app {X Y Z : C} (f : Z ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ X) (h : Z ⟶ X) (w : f ≫ g = h)
    (α : (presheafHom F G).obj (op X)) :
    ((presheafHom F G).map g.op α).app (op (Over.mk f)) =
      α.app (op (Over.mk h)) := by
  subst w
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma presheafHom_map_app_op_mk_id {X Y : C} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    (α : (presheafHom F G).obj (op X)) :
    dsimp% ((presheafHom F G).map g.op α).app (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y))) =
      α.app (op (Over.mk g)) :=
  presheafHom_map_app (𝟙 Y) g g (by simp) α

variable (F G)

/-- The sections of the presheaf `presheafHom F G` identify to morphisms `F ⟶ G`. -/
def presheafHomSectionsEquiv : (presheafHom F G).sections ≃ (F ⟶ G) where

def presheafHomSectionsEquiv : (presheafHom F G).sections ≃ (F ⟶ G) where
  toFun s :=
    { app := fun X => (s.1 X).app ⟨Over.mk (𝟙 _)⟩
      naturality := by
        rintro ⟨X₁⟩ ⟨X₂⟩ ⟨f : X₂ ⟶ X₁⟩
        dsimp
        refine Eq.trans ?_ ((s.1 ⟨X₁⟩).naturality
          (Over.homMk f : Over.mk f ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X₁)).op)
        rw [← s.2 f.op]
        dsimp
        rw [presheafHom_map_app_op_mk_id]
        rfl }
  invFun f := ⟨fun _ => Functor.whiskerLeft _ f, fun _ => rfl⟩
  left_inv s := by
    dsimp
    ext ⟨X⟩ ⟨Y : Over X⟩
    have H := s.2 Y.hom.op
    dsimp at H ⊢
    rw [← H]
    apply presheafHom_map_app_op_mk_id

variable {F G}

lemma PresheafHom.isAmalgamation_iff {X : C} (S : Sieve X)
    (x : Presieve.FamilyOfElements (presheafHom F G) S.arrows)
    (hx : x.Compatible) (y : (presheafHom F G).obj (op X)) :
    x.IsAmalgamation y ↔ ∀ (Y : C) (g : Y ⟶ X) (hg : S g),
      y.app (op (Over.mk g)) = (x g hg).app (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y))) := by
  constructor
  · intro h Y g hg
    rw [← h g hg]
    dsimp
    rw [presheafHom_map_app_op_mk_id]
  · intro h Y g hg
    dsimp
    ext ⟨W : Over Y⟩
    refine (h W.left (W.hom ≫ g) (S.downward_closed hg _)).trans ?_
    have H := hx (𝟙 _) W.hom (S.downward_closed hg W.hom) hg (by simp)
    simp only [op_id, Functor.map_id, id_apply] at H
    rw [H, presheafHom_map_app _ _ W.hom (by simp)]
    rfl

section

variable {X : C} {S : Sieve X}
    (hG : ∀ ⦃Y : C⦄ (f : Y ⟶ X), IsLimit (G.mapCone (S.pullback f).arrows.cocone.op))

namespace PresheafHom.IsSheafFor

variable (x : Presieve.FamilyOfElements (presheafHom F G) S.arrows) {Y : C}

include hG in
lemma exists_app (hx : x.Compatible) (g : Y ⟶ X) :
    ∃ (φ : F.obj (op Y) ⟶ G.obj (op Y)),
      ∀ {Z : C} (p : Z ⟶ Y) (hp : S (p ≫ g)), φ ≫ G.map p.op =
        F.map p.op ≫ (x (p ≫ g) hp).app ⟨Over.mk (𝟙 Z)⟩ := by
  let c : Cone ((Presieve.diagram (Sieve.pullback g S).arrows).op ⋙ G) :=
    { pt := F.obj (op Y)
      π :=
        { app := fun ⟨Z, hZ⟩ => F.map Z.hom.op ≫ (x _ hZ).app (op (Over.mk (𝟙 _)))
          naturality := by
            rintro ⟨Z₁, hZ₁⟩ ⟨Z₂, hZ₂⟩ ⟨⟨f : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁⟩⟩
            dsimp
            rw [id_comp, assoc]
            have H := hx f.left (𝟙 _) hZ₁ hZ₂ (by simp)
            simp only [op_id, Functor.map_id, id_apply] at H
            let φ : Over.mk f.left ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 Z₁.left) := Over.homMk f.left
            have H' := (x (Z₁.hom ≫ g) hZ₁).naturality φ.op
            dsimp at H H' ⊢
            erw [← H, ← H', presheafHom_map_app_op_mk_id, ← F.map_comp_assoc,
              ← op_comp, Over.w f] } }
  use (hG g).lift c
  intro Z p hp
  exact ((hG g).fac c ⟨Over.mk p, hp⟩)

/-- Auxiliary definition for `presheafHom_isSheafFor`. -/
noncomputable def app (hx : x.Compatible) (g : Y ⟶ X) : F.obj (op Y) ⟶ G.obj (op Y) :=
  (exists_app hG x hx g).choose

lemma app_cond (hx : x.Compatible) (g : Y ⟶ X) {Z : C} (p : Z ⟶ Y) (hp : S (p ≫ g)) :
    app hG x hx g ≫ G.map p.op = F.map p.op ≫ (x (p ≫ g) hp).app ⟨Over.mk (𝟙 Z)⟩ :=
  (exists_app hG x hx g).choose_spec p hp

end PresheafHom.IsSheafFor

variable (F G S)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
include hG in
open PresheafHom.IsSheafFor in
lemma presheafHom_isSheafFor :
    Presieve.IsSheafFor (presheafHom F G) S.arrows := by
  intro x hx
  apply existsUnique_of_exists_of_unique
  · refine ⟨
      { app := fun Y => app hG x hx Y.unop.hom
        naturality := by
          rintro ⟨Y₁ : Over X⟩ ⟨Y₂ : Over X⟩ ⟨φ : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁⟩
          apply (hG Y₂.hom).hom_ext
          rintro ⟨Z : Over Y₂.left, hZ⟩
          dsimp
          rw [assoc, assoc, app_cond hG x hx Y₂.hom Z.hom hZ, ← G.map_comp, ← op_comp]
          rw [app_cond hG x hx Y₁.hom (Z.hom ≫ φ.left) (by simpa using hZ),
            ← F.map_comp_assoc, op_comp]
          congr 3
          simp }, ?_⟩
    rw [PresheafHom.isAmalgamation_iff _ _ hx]
    intro Y g hg
    dsimp
    have H := app_cond hG x hx g (𝟙 _) (by simpa using hg)
    rw [op_id, G.map_id, comp_id, F.map_id, id_comp] at H
    exact H.trans (by congr; simp)
  · intro y₁ y₂ hy₁ hy₂
    rw [PresheafHom.isAmalgamation_iff _ _ hx] at hy₁ hy₂
    apply NatTrans.ext
    ext ⟨Y : Over X⟩
    apply (hG Y.hom).hom_ext
    rintro ⟨Z : Over Y.left, hZ⟩
    dsimp
    let φ : Over.mk (Z.hom ≫ Y.hom) ⟶ Y := Over.homMk Z.hom
    refine (y₁.naturality φ.op).symm.trans (Eq.trans ?_ (y₂.naturality φ.op))
    rw [(hy₁ _ _ hZ), ← ((hy₂ _ _ hZ))]

end

variable (F G)

lemma Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom (hG : Presheaf.IsSheaf J G) :
    Presheaf.IsSheaf J (presheafHom F G) := by
  rw [isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type]
  intro X S hS
  exact presheafHom_isSheafFor F G S
    (fun _ _ => ((Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isLimit J G).1 hG _ (J.pullback_stable _ hS)).some)


/-- The underlying presheaf of `sheafHom F G`. It is isomorphic to `presheafHom F.1 G.1`
(see `sheafHom'Iso`), but has better definitional properties. -/
def sheafHom' (F G : Sheaf J A) : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type _ where
  obj X := ((J.overPullback A X.unop).obj F ⟶ (J.overPullback A X.unop).obj G)
  map f := TypeCat.ofHom ((J.overMapPullback A f.unop).map)
  map_id X := by
This is the `presheafHom`/`Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` machinery the blueprint references. Let me look at `overSliceSheafEquiv` and the gluing API.

680	    refine ⟨((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W',
681	      ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).map a, ?_,
682	      ⟨y, hyW', rfl⟩⟩
683	    exact ⟨W', a, 𝟙 _, hSa, by simp⟩
684	
685	/-- The inverse of `overEquivalence U` exhibits `(Opens ↥U, subspace topology)` as a
686	dense subsite of `(Over U, slice topology)`. This is the dense-subsite datum
687	`Equivalence.sheafCongr` consumes; the only non-formal field is the pointwise
688	cover-correspondence `overEquiv_image_cover_iff`. -/
689	instance overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite :
690	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
691	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
692	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
693	  functorPushforward_mem_iff {W S} := by
694	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
695	    rw [show Sieve.overEquiv ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W)
696	          (S.functorPushforward (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse)
697	        = S.functorPushforward
698	            ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U) by
699	      rw [Sieve.functorPushforward_comp]; rfl]
700	    exact overEquiv_image_cover_iff U W S
701	
702	/-- **The open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site equivalence.** For a topological space
703	`X`, an open `U : Opens X`, and any value category `A`, the slice sheaf category
704	over `U` is equivalent to the sheaf category on the open subspace `↥U`:
705	
706	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
707	
708	This completes the documented Mathlib `## TODO` at `Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` and
709	is the SHARED root both ⊗-inverse bridges (`homOfLocalCompat`, `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
710	reduce to. Built by transporting `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U` across
711	`CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, using the project-local dense-subsite
712	instance `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`.
713	
714	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
715	noncomputable def overSliceSheafEquiv (A : Type w) [Category.{v} A] :
716	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌
717	      Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
718	  (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
719	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
720	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
721	
722	end OverSliceSheafEquiv
723	
724	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

227	/-- If a family `B` of open sets forms a basis of the topology on `X`, and if `F'`
228	is a sheaf on `X`, then a homomorphism between a presheaf `F` on `X` and `F'`
229	is equivalent to a homomorphism between their restrictions to the indexing type
230	`ι` of `B`, with the induced category structure on `ι`. -/
231	def restrictHomEquivHom (h : Opens.IsBasis (Set.range B)) :
232	    ((inducedFunctor B).op ⋙ F ⟶ (inducedFunctor B).op ⋙ F'.1) ≃ (F ⟶ F'.1) :=
233	  @Functor.IsCoverDense.restrictHomEquivHom _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _

238	    (α : (inducedFunctor B).op ⋙ F ⟶ (inducedFunctor B).op ⋙ F'.1) (i : ι) :
239	    (restrictHomEquivHom F F' h α).app (op (B i)) = α.app (op i) := by
240	  nth_rw 2 [← (restrictHomEquivHom F F' h).left_inv α]
241	  rfl
242	
243	theorem hom_ext (h : Opens.IsBasis (Set.range B))
244	    {α β : F ⟶ F'.1} (he : ∀ i, α.app (op (B i)) = β.app (op (B i))) : α = β := by
245	  apply (restrictHomEquivHom F F' h).symm.injective
246	  ext i
247	  exact he i.unop

343	/-! ## §C. The A-bridge: compatible local morphisms glue to a global morphism -/
344	
345	/-- **A-bridge: compatible local `𝒪_X`-module morphisms glue to a global morphism.**
346	
347	Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
348	`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
349	morphism `f i : M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι` in `Scheme.Modules (U i)` such
350	that for all `i j` the restrictions of `f i` and `f j` to `U i ⊓ U j` are equal (as
351	morphisms in `Scheme.Modules (U i ⊓ U j)`, compared via `HEq` since the double-restriction
352	routes give propositionally but not definitionally equal source/target objects), then there
353	is a unique global morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.
354

input.old_string: /-! ## §C. The A-bridge: compatible local morphisms glue to a global morphism -/
input.new_string:
/-! ## §C. The A-bridge: compatible local morphisms glue to a global morphism -/

open Opposite TopologicalSpace in
/-- **The local section of the hom-sheaf manufactured from `f i`** (the load-bearing piece
of `homOfLocalCompat`, blueprint `localSection`).  Working with the underlying `Ab`-presheaves
`F = M.val.presheaf`, `G = N.val.presheaf`, the presheaf of types
`presheafHom F G` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) sends an open `W` to the morphisms of
the restrictions of `F`, `G` to the slice `Over W`.  Its value at `U i` is built from the
components of `f i`, conjugated by `eqToHom` along the down-set identity
`(U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` (valid for `V ≤ U i`).  The naturality field — the genuine
coherence risk — is automatic on the thin poset `Opens X` once the `eqToHom`-conjugation is
peeled, via `Subsingleton.elim` on the hom-sets. -/
noncomputable def homLocalSection {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
  app W :=
    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle
    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg.symm)) ≫
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
  naturality := by sorry

input.content:
import Mathlib

set_option autoImplicit false
universe u
open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

noncomputable def homLocalSection {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
  app W :=
    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle
    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg.symm)) ≫
      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
  naturality := by sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

    M ⟶ N := by
  -- Step (i): glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.  The morphisms-presheaf
  -- `presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) is a
  -- sheaf of types because `N` is a sheaf (`Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom`, consuming `N.isSheaf`).
  let H : TopCat.Sheaf (Type u) (X : TopCat) :=
    ⟨CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf,
      Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf N.isSheaf⟩
  -- The cover `{U i}` exhausts `X`, so `iSup U = ⊤`.
  have hsup : iSup U = ⊤ := by
    rw [eq_top_iff]
    intro x _
    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hU x
    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨i, hi⟩
  -- The compatible family `homLocalSection U f` (its naturality is the load-bearing field,
  -- proved axiom-clean above) glues via `existsUnique_gluing` to a unique global section of `H`
  -- over `iSup U = ⊤`.  `hglue` records the unique-gluing engine fed with these local sections;

## homLocalSection (NEW, CLOSED axiom-clean)

- **`app W`** (the component): `M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom h.symm) ≫ ((toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.left)) ≫ N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom h)`,
  where `h : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.left) = W.left` (from `Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf` + `inf_eq_right.mpr W.hom.le`).
  Crucial defeq used: `restrict_obj`/`restrict_map` are `rfl`, so `(M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.obj/map`
  is **definitionally** `M.val.presheaf.{obj,map}` at the image open.
- **`naturality`** (the load-bearing field): reduced to the naturality `hm` of
  `(toPresheaf _).map (f i).val`, conjugated by two **thin-poset `Subsingleton.elim`** edge
  equalities `hsubM`/`hsubN` (any two parallel maps in `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ` are equal), packaged as
  `hML` (M-side) and `hNR` (N-side) via `Functor.map_comp`.

### KEY IDIOMS / DEAD-ENDS (carrier-friction wall — record for reuse)
- The composites mix `M.val.presheaf.{obj,map}` (image form) with `(M.restrict …).val.presheaf`
  (restrict form) at the `((toPresheaf).map (f i).val).app` boundary; these are **defeq but not
  syntactic**. This breaks `rw`/`simp`/`slice` on `Functor.map_comp` and `Category.assoc`
  *asymmetrically* (one side merges, the other "pattern not found"; `Category.assoc` silently
  rewrites the *other* side).
- WORKING RECIPE (verified):
  1. State `hML` with RHS in **restrict form** `(M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.map κ.op`
     (so its boundary with `m_preB` is syntactic), prove it with
     `rw [(F.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]; exact F.map_comp _ _` (NOT `← Functor.map_comp` twice —
     the second occurrence fails to match).
  2. State `hNR` in **pure `N.val.presheaf` form** and apply it with **`erw`** (defeq-matching)
     in the main chain.
  3. Main chain: `rw [← Category.assoc, hML]; erw [Category.assoc, reassoc_of% hm, hNR]; simp only [Category.assoc]; rfl`.
     `erw` is essential for the assoc step across the restrict/image boundary; the final
     `simp only [Category.assoc]` + `rfl` discharges the 4-fold associativity + `eqToHom`
     proof-irrelevance residual.
- DEAD: plain `rw [Functor.map_comp]` / `simp only [Functor.map_comp]` forward-split (never matches
  the `opensFunctor`-image factor); `slice_lhs` (treats a defeq-boundary 2-comp as one atomic
  factor); term-mode `congrArg F.map (Subsingleton.elim _ _)` inside a `.trans` chain (whnf
  timeout — Subsingleton args underdetermined).

## homOfLocalCompat (scaffold + sorry)
Verified-compiling scaffold:
```
let H : TopCat.Sheaf (Type u) X := ⟨presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf,
  Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom _ _ N.isSheaf⟩
have hsup : iSup U = ⊤ := …            -- from hU, via Opens.mem_iSup
have hglue := H.existsUnique_gluing U (fun i => homLocalSection U f i)
sorry
```
Remaining residual (the next-iter work, all reduced to standard gluing bookkeeping):
- **(a)** `IsCompatible H.1 U (homLocalSection U f)` — feed `hglue`; this is exactly `hf`
  (HEq overlap agreement of the two double-restrictions on `U i ⊓ U j`). Likely needs the
  `PresheafHom.isAmalgamation_iff`/sectionwise comparison + transporting `hf`'s HEq through the
  `eqToHom`-conjugation in `homLocalSection`.
- **(b)** terminal-section → morphism: the glued section lives in `H.1.obj (op (iSup U))` with
  `iSup U = ⊤`; `⊤` is terminal in `Opens X`, so this is `≅ (M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)`
  (cf. `presheafHomSectionsEquiv`, which is stated for `.sections` — need the `op ⊤ ↔ .sections`
  bridge, or hand-build `g.app W := s.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE le_top)))`).
- **(c)** sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linearity `hg` of `g` (holds on each `U i` since `g|_{U i}` is the
  module map `f i`; ambient presheaf separated ⇒ global), then `Scheme.Modules.homMk g hg : M ⟶ N`.

## dual_restrict_iso Step-4 (L256) — NOT attempted this iter
Budget went to Step A (PRIMARY `homOfLocalCompat`). Plan unchanged (dual252): leg (A)
`sliceDualTransport` (standalone first) + leg (B) `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv`, OR the
inverse-uniqueness shortcut from `tensorObj_restrict_iso`.
